Confidential

Asking Caitlyn
Mensah

To be my girlfriend — surrounded by everyone she loves

Holland Park · W11 3:30 — 5:30pm Faith-centred 30–50 Guests Free Entry
Scroll

Overview

Concept & Cover Story

She thinks she is heading to a picnic date with a brother. What she doesn't know is that she's walking straight into me asking her to be my girlfriend — surrounded by everyone she loves, in one of London's most beautiful gardens.

The Theme
Jeremiah 29:11–13

"For I know the plans I have for you," declares the Lord, "plans to prosper you and not to harm you, plans to give you hope and a future. Then you will call on me and come and pray to me, and I will listen to you. You will seek me and find me when you seek me with all your heart."

The Cover Story
A casual picnic date

She is told she is going on a casual picnic date with a brother (Group D). She has no reason to suspect anything — it is framed as a relaxed afternoon out.

Group D walks her into Holland Park's formal gardens where everyone is already waiting.

2 · Location

Holland Park —
Formal Gardens

Free entry. One of London's most cinematic outdoor spaces. The Dutch Garden lawn is your centre stage.

VenueHolland Park Formal Gardens (Dutch Garden lawn area)
AddressHolland Park Ave, London W11 4UA
EntryFree — no booking required
Opening hours7:30am – 8:00pm daily
Nearest tubeHolland Park (Central Line) · Kensington Olympia (Mildmay & District Line)
Centre stageThe open flat lawn beside the Dutch Garden hedgerows
After photosKyoto Garden waterfall & koi pond — 3 min walk
Open in Google Maps →

3 · Team

Group Roles &
Responsibilities

Four groups, one seamless plan. Everyone has a role. Everyone matters.

A
Friends & Family
30–50 community members, close friends, family
Arrive at the venue by 3:00pm. Already in position when she arrives for 3:30pm. Form a welcoming crowd around the centre stage. Cheer, celebrate, and capture personal photos.
B
Gift Bearers
3 Sisters + supporting sisters
Positioned at the edge of the centre stage. Each sister steps forward during the corresponding part of the poem to present their gift.
Sis 1 · Scrapbook Sis 2 · Polaroid Sis 3 · Flowers
C
Media Team
Hired photographer + videographer
Photographer positioned discreetly before she arrives. Videographer captures the walk-in wide. Media team splits: one on her face, one on you. All gifts and poem moments are documented. Capture crowd reactions too.
D
The Decoy Date
Brothers and sisters on the date
Run the date as normal. Meet up location could be the train station. Stay in contact via WhatsApp with the group throughout. Lead her in naturally — no over-explaining.

4 · Timeline

Step-by-Step
Run of Day

2:30pm
You arrive & decorate
Set up centre stage — blanket/rug, rose petals, basket with gifts. Brief Group C on angles and positions.
3:00pm
Group A & B arrive
All friends and family take their positions. Group B sisters have gifts ready. Group C in filming position.
3:15pm
Group D departs
Date meet up — the brother leads the date as normal. Stay in contact via WhatsApp with the group.
3:25pm
Final check
You stand at centre stage. Confirm everyone is quiet and in position. Group C rolls on video.
3:30pm
She arrives at the park entrance
Group D walks her through the park path. Crowd stays silent until she rounds the corner.
3:35pm
⭐ The reveal
She sees the crowd and realises — this is not a date. The moment of realisation is captured on camera. Everyone keeps quiet, music is being played.
3:38pm
She comes to the front
She comes to the area where I am. Music still playing.
3:40pm
⭐ The poem & gift sequence
You deliver your poem. At key moments: Sis 1 steps forward with the scrapbook, Sis 2 with the Polaroid, Sis 3 with flowers.
3:52pm
⭐ The ask
Final lines of the poem lead into the question. You ask her to be your girlfriend.
3:53pm
⭐ She says yes
Crowd erupts. Group C captures everything. Embrace, celebrate, enjoy the moment.
4:00pm
Celebration portraits
Photographer leads couple to Kyoto Garden waterfall for post-asking photos.
5:30pm
Close
Pack down. Group heads off. Myself and new girlfriend go get some food at an Italian restaurant or do a picnic date with a couple of people.

5 · Setup

Decor & Centre Stage

The garden provides the backdrop — your decor should enhance it, not compete with it. Elegant, natural, intentional.

🧺
Large Picnic Blanket / Rug
Sage green, ivory, or blush — laid at the centre of the lawn as your stage.
🌹
Rose Petals
Scattered in a loose heart shape or path leading to your spot.
🧺
Wicker Basket
Contains scrapbook & Polaroid — presented by sisters during the poem.
💐
Flowers — Sis 3
Fresh bouquet, white and blush tones to match the garden palette.
🐘
Elephant Keepsake
Placed inside the basket — a personal nod to what she loves.
📜
Printed Poem Card
Optional: laminated copy of your poem as a keepsake for her to keep forever.

6 · Media

Filming & Photography Brief

Brief Group C on every shot. The best footage is planned, not accidental.

1
Wide establishing shot — full garden setup before she arrives
2
Her face as she rounds the corner and sees the crowd (the reveal)
3
Over-the-shoulder — you leading her to centre stage
4
Close-up of her face during each poem movement
5
Each sister stepping forward with her gift
6
Her reaction when she realises you're asking
7
The full crowd reaction when she says yes
8
The embrace — full-body wide shot from a distance
9
Post-asking pictures: Kyoto Garden waterfall couple portraits

Positioning Notes

Videographer: wide, static angle from behind the crowd — capture everything
Photographer: mobile — move between the crowd, her face, and your face
One person dedicated to filming the walk-in from the park entrance
One person among Group D discreetly filming her reaction on approach

Our Story

The moments
that led here.

Candid. Genuine. Ours.

Pure joy, no filter needed.
The laugh that got me.
When we clean up well.
Sunshine suits her.

7 · Checklist

Day-Of Preparation
Checklist

Tick each one off as you go. Tap the boxes to mark complete.

The Week Before
Confirm the date and check the weather forecast
Brief all four groups on their roles — share this document
Finalise and rehearse your poem
Purchase / prepare all gifts (scrapbook, Polaroid, elephant keepsake)
Order / buy rose petals and flowers
Create the music playlist and test the speaker
Brief Group D (the decoy brother) with full instructions
Day Of
Arrive at Holland Park by 2:30pm with all decor
Set up centre stage — rug, petals, basket
Brief Group C on shot list and positions
Confirm Group A & B are in position by 3:00pm
Confirm Group D has left to collect her
Take a deep breath. Be present. You've got this.
